app.title: Logic Gates — Interactive Digital Logic
app.subtitle: From transistors to computation: how gates process information
fundamental.intro: Logic gates are the building blocks of all digital circuits. Each gate performs a simple Boolean operation on one or two binary inputs. What makes them remarkable is that every gate is physically built from just a few CMOS transistors on silicon.
gate.not.name: NOT (Inverter)
gate.not.description: Outputs the opposite of its input. The simplest possible gate — just 2 transistors (1 PMOS + 1 NMOS). When input is 1, NMOS pulls output to ground. When input is 0, PMOS pulls output to Vdd.
gate.and.name: AND
gate.and.description: Outputs 1 only when BOTH inputs are 1. Built as NAND + NOT (6 transistors). Not a 'natural' CMOS gate — it needs an extra inverter stage because CMOS naturally inverts.
gate.or.name: OR
gate.or.description: Outputs 1 when EITHER input is 1. Built as NOR + NOT (6 transistors). Like AND, it's not natural in CMOS and requires an extra inverter.
gate.xor.name: XOR (Exclusive OR)
gate.xor.description: Outputs 1 when inputs DIFFER. The most complex basic gate (8-12 transistors). Essential for arithmetic — XOR computes the sum bit in binary addition.
truthTable.title: Truth Table
